entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on lamentations 3:39
entries.excerpt: A hermit said, 'In every trial do not blame other people but blame yourself, saying, "This has happened to me because of my sins." '

entries.title: Commentary on Lamentations on lamentations 3:39
entries.excerpt: Second, arguments are advanced regarding divine power against persons who deride the above ideas, through impatience within murmurings. "Why should a living man complain, a man, about the punishment of his sins?" Namely, as to his known sins, from which he now suffers. For, Wisdom 1:11 declares: "Beware then of useless murmuring, and keep your tongue from slander."
